title: centaurea carellii
intro: Centaurea carellii, also known as mountain cornflower or alpine knapweed, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Centaurea carellii include its ability to soothe and calm the body, reducing stress and anxiety, and its potential to support the immune system. Centaurea carellii is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
